title: Cass McCombs & Hand Habits Announce Split 7”: Hear “Seeing The Elephant”
author: Danielle Chelosky
summary:
Cass McCombs recently kicked off his tour to bring last year’s Interior Live Oak to the stage. In New York, he was introduced by comedian Adam Friedland, who called him “the greatest singer-songwriter in the world.” Now he’s announcing a split 7″ with Hand Habits, who’s opening for him at some of the upcoming shows.…

The post Cass McCombs & Hand Habits Announce Split 7”: Hear “Seeing The Elephant” appeared first on Stereogum.
full_text:
Cass McCombs recently kicked off his tour to bring last year's Interior Live Oak to the stage. In New York, he was introduced by comedian Adam Friedland, who called him "the greatest singer-songwriter in the world." Now he's announcing a split 7" with Hand Habits, who's opening for him at some of the upcoming shows. 

The 7" will be available exclusively on the tour. Meg Duffy's last record was August's Blue Reminder, and they've shared collaborations with Mirah and Morgan Nagler since. Their track is called "Good Person," but it's McCombs' "Seeing The Elephant" that's out today. The energetic little tune comes with a music video directed by Larry Unrein, and it has lots of elephants. Watch below.
